titleEn: Participation in the 22nd International Conference on Technology, Knowledge and Society
excerptEn: The University of the Aegean hosted the twenty-second edition of the International Conference on Technology, Knowledge and Society on 16 and 17 April 2026 in Rhodes (Greece). The event was held bilingually (Spanish and English) in a hybrid format combining on-site attendance with virtual participation, structured around…
contentEn:
The University of the Aegean hosted the twenty-second edition of the International Conference on Technology, Knowledge and Society on 16 and 17 April 2026 in Rhodes (Greece).

The event was held bilingually (Spanish and English) in a hybrid format combining on-site attendance with virtual participation, and was structured around four thematic strands: histories of technology; knowledge makers; social and cultural realities and interculturality; and curriculum, assessment and technologies in education.

The University Institute of Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca presented the paper “LLM-assisted assessment: cross-validation between ChatGPT and university teaching staff”, authored by Susana Olmos-Migueláñez, Marina García-Herrero and Alberto Ortiz-López, representing the University of Salamanca, together with Gregorio Rodríguez-Gómez and María Soledad Ibarra-Sáiz, of the University of Cádiz.

titleEn: The training activities of the 2026 Teaching Staff Training Plan get under way in April
excerptEn: With more than 100 activities scheduled, the 2026 Teaching Staff Training Plan — the largest in recent years — is now under way. Within the General Training Programme, the course Design and evaluation of teaching innovation projects at the University took place on 10 April, a workshop taught by Susana…
contentEn:
With more than 100 activities scheduled, the 2026 Teaching Staff Training Plan — the largest in recent years — is now under way.

Within the General Training Programme, the course Design and evaluation of teaching innovation projects at the University took place on 10 April — a workshop taught by Susana Álvarez Álvarez (University of Valladolid) and Ignacio de Miguel Jiménez (University of Valladolid) which had the honour of opening the training activities of the programme.

In addition, several of the courses within Module 5 on information literacy have also been successfully delivered, such as course 26505 on scientific publishing, taught by Almudena Mangas and Sonia Santiago; course 26508 on the open-access model of scientific publishing, taught by Lourdes Palacios and Diego Martín; and course 26506 on research data management, taught by Sonia Santiago.

As for online training, the course Participatory feedback for formative assessment, taught by Professor María Elena Cano García (University of Barcelona), ran intensively during the week of 13 to 17 April. Among the other online training activities, the workshop Designing and setting up quizzes in Studium has also begun: participants will work independently until mid-May to complete the activities, with the ongoing support of the teaching team, Fernando Martínez and Juan Pablo Hernández.

Turning to the In-Centre Training Programme, in addition to the activities brought forward to January, highlights include the workshop Implementing learning outcomes and developing course guides at the Faculty of Geography and History, taught by Professor Mónica Gutiérrez Ortega (University of Valladolid), and From idea to viva: a workshop on planning, supervising and assessing final-year projects for teaching staff, held at the Faculty of Education Sciences in Zamora and delivered by several members of the centre's teaching staff: Diego Corrochano, Javier Cruz, Judit García, Luis González and María Teresa del Moral.

Across all the activities, attendance and engagement have been remarkably high, with attendance and completion rates above 90%.

titleEn: The USAL at the 22nd International Conference on Technology, Knowledge and Society
excerptEn: The University of the Aegean hosted the 2026 edition of the conference on the island of Rhodes — bilingual, in a hybrid format, and with the participation of IUCE researchers.
contentEn:
The University of the Aegean was the host institution of the 22nd International Conference on Technology, Knowledge and Society, held on 16 and 17 April 2026 on the island of Rhodes (Greece), in a hybrid format and with a bilingual programme.

The conference brought together researchers from more than twenty countries around the challenges technology poses to contemporary society: artificial intelligence in educational contexts, the digital divide, citizenship and the governance of knowledge.

“The conversation about educational technology is no longer about tools, but about evidence: what actually improves learning, and under what conditions.”

IUCE researchers contributed several papers on technology-mediated educational assessment and learning analytics, in line with the active projects of the Institute and of the GRIAL group.

The next edition of the conference will take place in 2027, at a venue yet to be confirmed. The proceedings will be published in open access over the coming months.

titleEn: Participation in the International Conference on Educational Assessment
excerptEn: On 16 and 17 March 2026, Bilbao hosted the first edition of the International Conference on Educational Assessment, organised by the Basque Institute for Educational Evaluation and Research (ISEI-IVEI) together with the Department of Education of the Basque Government. Designed for the education community, the conference explored the potential of…
contentEn:
On 16 and 17 March 2026, Bilbao hosted the first edition of the International Conference on Educational Assessment, organised by the Basque Institute for Educational Evaluation and Research (ISEI-IVEI) together with the Department of Education of the Basque Government. Designed for the education community, the conference explored the potential of assessment to drive educational change and improvement. The University Institute of Education Sciences of the University of Salamanca presented two posters:

“EQGED: an indicator of gender equity in education across the Autonomous Communities”, by Elena Sáez-González, Adriana Gamazo, José Carlos Sánchez-Prieto and Susana Olmos-Migueláñez.

“Educational inequity in the Basque Country and Castilla y León: a study based on PISA 2022”, by Marina García-Herrero, María Holgado-Aguadero, Cristina Frade-Martínez and María-José Rodríguez-Conde.

titleEn: 1st International Conference on Educational Assessment, in Bilbao
excerptEn: Organised by ISEI-IVEI together with the Department of Education of the Basque Government.
contentEn:
On 19 and 20 March Bilbao hosted the 1st International Conference on Educational Assessment, organised by the Basque Institute for Educational Evaluation and Research (ISEI-IVEI) together with the Department of Education of the Basque Government.

IUCE researchers took part with papers on the diagnostic assessment of competences and the use of evidence in education policy.

titleEn: IUCE members take part in the 3rd Teaching Innovation Conference of the Public Universities of Castilla y León (JIDUCYL) in Burgos
excerptEn: The University of Salamanca has strengthened its leadership in educational innovation after taking part in the 3rd Teaching Innovation Conference of the Public Universities of Castilla y León (JIDUCYL26), held on 13 March in Burgos. Under the motto “From experience to evidence”, the event brought together more than 300 experts to discuss new…
contentEn:
The University of Salamanca has strengthened its leadership in educational innovation after taking part in the 3rd Teaching Innovation Conference of the Public Universities of Castilla y León (JIDUCYL26), held on 13 March in Burgos. Under the motto “From experience to evidence”, the event brought together more than 300 experts to discuss new teaching methodologies.

The Salamanca delegation comprised 75 lecturers and researchers, among them members of the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE). The Institute coordinated the USAL's participation within the 2026 Teaching Staff Training Plan — the event itself is part of the Plan — and its experts joined 19 idea labs and 8 specialised workshops aimed at turning the classroom into a more dynamic and effective learning space.

The Institute and the University also play a key role in the regional working group FIUniCyL, through which the USAL maintains an ongoing collaboration with the universities of Burgos, León and Valladolid to exchange good practice and support the initial and continuing training of teaching staff. Thanks to this sustained work in training and innovation, Salamanca has taken over from Burgos and will host the 4th Teaching Innovation Conference in 2028.

titleEn: The USAL at the 3rd JIDUCYL26 Teaching Innovation Conference
excerptEn: Under the motto “From experience to evidence”, Burgos brought together more than 300 lecturers from the public universities of Castilla y León.
contentEn:
The University of Burgos hosted, on 13 March, the 3rd Teaching Innovation Conference of the public universities of Castilla y León (JIDUCYL26), under the motto “From experience to evidence”.

More than 300 lecturers from the region's four public universities shared innovation experiences backed by evidence. The USAL delegation, coordinated by the IUCE, presented papers on formative assessment, flipped classrooms and learning analytics.

The next edition will be held in 2027 at the University of León.

titleEn: The IUCE's 2025 activity report has been published
excerptEn: A year of research, training and knowledge transfer, in figures.
contentEn:
The IUCE has published its 2025 activity report, which captures in figures a year of research, training and knowledge transfer to the education system.

The document reviews the active projects, the activities of the Teaching Training Plan, the scientific output of the research groups and the activity of the EKS journal.

titleEn: The 2026 Teaching Staff Training Plan gets under way
excerptEn: With more than 100 activities scheduled, it is the largest plan in recent years.
contentEn:
The IUCE is launching the 2026 Teaching Training Plan, an initiative of the Vice-Rectorate for Undergraduate Studies and Quality of the University of Salamanca.

With more than a hundred activities scheduled — courses, workshops, seminars and SPOCs — it is the largest plan in recent years. The programme covers teaching methodologies, assessment, educational technology, languages and research data management.

The full programme and registration are available in the Training section of this website and on the institutional platform.

titleEn: Ennova Health Award for the VisualMed System group
excerptEn: Presented at the Real Teatro del Retiro in Madrid.
contentEn:
The VisualMed System research group, affiliated with the IUCE and led by Juan Antonio Juanes, has received the 2025 Ennova Health Award for its work on medical visualisation and technologies applied to training in the Health Sciences.

The award was presented at a ceremony held at the Real Teatro del Retiro in Madrid, which brought together the most innovative projects in the healthcare and education fields.

titleEn: The VisualMed System research group wins the 2025 Ennova Health Award in the Remote monitoring devices category
excerptEn: On 19 November, the Real Teatro del Retiro in Madrid hosted the presentation of the Ennova Health Award to the VisualMed System group. The award, in the Remote monitoring devices category, recognises the work entitled “Sensorised immobilisation splints”. The project stems from the doctoral thesis of José María de Agustín…
contentEn:
On 19 November, the Real Teatro del Retiro in Madrid hosted the presentation of the Ennova Health Award to the VisualMed System group. The award, in the Remote monitoring devices category, recognises the work entitled “Sensorised immobilisation splints”.

The project stems from the doctoral thesis of José María de Agustín del Burgo, carried out within the Education in the Knowledge Society programme and supervised by Professors Fernando Blaya Haro and Juan Antonio Juanes Méndez, the latter being the director of the VisualMed group. The project also benefited from the collaboration of Dr Francisco Javier de Toro Santos, of the University of A Coruña, and Alonso Blaya San Pedro, of the Centre for Bioengineering, Posture and Health in Madrid.

The team has successfully incorporated sensors and treatment windows into the splints they designed, without compromising the rigidity required for their primary purpose as immobilisation splints. Several sensors have been implemented to record, in real time, a range of data on the evolution of the injury — including temperature and pressure at several points, humidity, skin colour and splint-placement detection — making it possible to describe them as “smart splints”. All the electronics have been integrated into an autonomous, portable and lightweight system that gathers the data from the different sensors in real time.

titleEn: Renewal of the Governing Board of the Spanish Pedagogy Society (SEP)
excerptEn: On 19 November 2025, in the Assembly Hall of the Faculty of Education of the UNED and in a hybrid format, the renewal of the Governing Board took place within the General Assembly of the Spanish Pedagogy Society. The IUCE candidates, Fernando Martínez Abad (treasurer) and María José Rodríguez Conde…
contentEn:
On 19 November 2025, in the Assembly Hall of the Faculty of Education of the UNED and in a hybrid format, the renewal of the Governing Board took place within the General Assembly of the Spanish Pedagogy Society. The IUCE candidates, Fernando Martínez Abad (treasurer) and María José Rodríguez Conde (president), were elected unanimously.

The Spanish Pedagogy Society (SEP) is the oldest scientific society in the field of Education in Spain, a founding member of the European Educational Research Association (EERA) as well as of WERA. Founded in 1949 by Víctor García Hoz and a small group of collaborators, it is a non-profit scientific and professional organisation, open to everyone interested in reflecting on education and improving its quality. In 2025, for the first time, the presidency goes outside Madrid and to a woman. With this election, the members of the IUCE — in this case of the Ge2o group within the GRIAL group — consolidate and further advance their leadership in educational research at the national level.

With this appointment, the University Institute of Education Sciences (IUCE) strengthens its position as a centre of excellence and a benchmark on the educational research scene. The presence of its members in the leadership of the SEP boosts the national reach of the research lines and trajectories developed from Salamanca, highlighting the quality and impact of its research groups.

On 19 November 2025, following the vote, the current Governing Board of the SEP is made up of the following members:

Executive Committee

Institutional legacy

The new presidency takes over from major figures of Spanish pedagogy:

  • 1949-1981: Víctor García Hoz
  • 1981-2000: Arturo de la Orden Hoz
  • 2001-2008: Ramón Pérez Juste
  • 2008-2016: José Luis Gaviria Soto
  • 2016-2025: Gonzalo Jover Olmeda

Further information and links of interest:

  • Spanish Pedagogy Society (SEP)
  • European Educational Research Association (EERA)
  • World Education Research Association (WERA)
